title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 1 - Introduction and Buyer's Guide
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KEnYv8bMxXM

Daniel (@dungeondive) launches an ambitious deep dive series into Shadows of Brimstone, the sprawling dungeon crawl from Flying Frog Productions. This opening episode doubles as a buyer's guide for newcomers overwhelmed by the sheer volume of available content.

The recommended starting point: pick up two core sets—Forbidden Fortress and Swamps of Death—for around $75–95 each. This gives you eight heroes, four different environments, and a solid mix of Eastern and Western settings. From there, add the Frontier Town expansion for a richer settlement phase, one Otherworld deluxe expansion matched to your preferred theme (sci-fi, post-apocalyptic, Weird War II, or Eastern horror), and start building out your enemy variety with deluxe enemy packs like the Scafford Gang, Undead Outlaws, and Serpentmen.

Daniel's target budget is roughly $500 spread over a few months—about the cost of three large Kickstarters—which he argues delivers two years of gameplay variety. He recommends buying piecemeal at retail rather than backing massive Kickstarter pledges, and watching for Flying Frog's quarterly web store sales. The one caveat: mini assembly is required, and the quality of the earlier sculpts can be frustrating.

----

What's your preferred approach to big hobby games—all in at once, or build the collection gradually?
